summary: Added an observable-only M7 platform/resolution/localization matrix in tests/e2e/web-shell.spec.ts plus a JSON fixture under tests/e2e. The Chromium Playwright coverage now records supported browser, desktop smoke, supported resolution, and locale rows, and it keeps Edge, Firefox, and Windows DPI 150% as explicit unsupported follow-ups instead of pretending they are covered.

tests_run.result: Listed active tasks; M7-PLATFORM-RESOLUTION-LOCALIZATION-MATRIX-001 is in progress.

tests_run.command: node -e "const fs=require('node:fs');const p='project/tasks/active/M7-PLATFORM-RESOLUTION-LOCALIZATION-MATRIX-001.json';const j=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(p,'utf8'));if(j.id!=='M7-PLATFORM-RESOLUTION-LOCALIZATION-MATRIX-001') throw new Error('bad id');if(j.status!=='IN_PROGRESS') throw new Error('bad status '+j.status);if(j.route_to!=='qa_reviewer') throw new Error('bad route_to '+j.route_to);if(!Array.isArray(j.acceptance_criteria)||j.acceptance_criteria.length<5) throw new Error('bad acceptance');console.log('shape-ok')"

tests_run.result: Vite web build completed successfully with chunk-size warnings.

Formatted tests/e2e/web-shell.spec.ts; matrix fixture was unchanged.
Playwright chromium suite passed: 23 tests, 23 passed.
Electron security preferences passed; allowed IPC channels are desktop:getRuntimeInfo and desktop:setFullscreen.
Packaged main, preload, renderer, and platform adapter artifacts into .tmp/desktop-package/MonsoonSovereigns-win32-x64.
No whitespace or patch formatting issues.
Initial attempt failed because Prettier flagged tests/e2e/web-shell.spec.ts.
format, lint, typecheck, and architecture checks passed.

decisions.result: Kept the matrix close to the existing web-shell Playwright coverage and anchored the supported rows in a JSON fixture so the assertions stay observable-only.

Recorded Edge and Firefox as blocked-by-tooling because this workspace's Playwright config only defines chromium, and recorded Windows DPI 150% as a follow-up because there is no dedicated DPI matrix harness.
Switched the fixture loader from import.meta.url to process.cwd() after the first Playwright attempt proved this spec is being loaded through a CJS path in the current tooling.

risks: Edge and Firefox remain explicit unsupported rows until the workspace grows a non-chromium Playwright matrix.
Windows DPI 150% remains a follow-up because there is still no dedicated DPI harness in the current browser/desktop smoke tooling.

The supported locale matrix shows the UI-visible zh-Hans/en-US labels while the fixture normalizes expectedLang to zh-CN for system and zh-CN rows; QA should confirm that this normalization matches the intended locale policy.
requested_action: Review the new platform/resolution/localization matrix evidence. Accept if the Chromium and desktop-supported rows plus the explicit unsupported follow-ups are sufficient, or request changes with exact failing evidence.
